properties.remark: SEVERAL ROADS IN AND AROUND HARSTELLE ARE BECOMING IMPASSABLE DUE TO RISING WATER.

WATER REPORTED COVERING LITTLE COVE ROAD IN THE KILLINGSWORTH COVE AREA OF GURLEY. ROAD IS NOT CLOSED AT THIS TIME.
WATER REPORTED OVER THE ROADWAY ON CANNON ROAD. OTHER REPORTS OF WATER COVERING THE ROADWAY ON SEVERAL ROADS THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY.
UPDATE. LITTLE COVE ROAD IN GURLEY IS IMPASSABLE AT THIS TIME. THERE IS ALSO A CAR STRANDED IN THE AREA...BUT IT IS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME IF THE TWO EVENTS ARE RELATED.
ROCK CUT ROAD IN GURLEY IS BARRICADED AND IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOOD WATERS OVER THE ROAD.

AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH AND A PATH WIDTH OF 20 YARDS TRACKED ABOUT ONE QUARTER MILE ON A FARM JUST EAST OF BATTLEGROUND. THE TORNADO FIRST SNAPPED AND TWISTED FOUR TALL HARDWOOD TREES BEFORE ADVANCING NORTHEAST AND DESTROYING A LARGE BUT UNOCCUPIED CHICKEN BARN. METAL AND WOOD DEBRIS WAS SHREDDED AND TWISTED AMONGST NEARBY TREES AND DAMAGED THE ADJACENT HOME AND A MOBILE HOME.
REPORTS OF PINE AND OAK TREES DOWN...POWER POLES DOWN AND SKIRTING TORN OFF A MOBILE HOME ON GARNER ROAD.
WINDOWS WERE BLOWN OUT IN A MOBILE HOME.
TREE DOWN ON RESIDENCE ON FORREST CHAPEL ROAD IN OAKRIDGE.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 95 MPH...PATH LENGTH OF 4.2 MILES...AND PATH WIDTH OF 200 YDS TOUCHED DOWN ALONG BORDERLINE RD BETWEEN EVERETT JONES RD AND GREENVILLE PK. THE TORNADO DEMOLISHED MULTIPLE SHEDS...SNAPPED AND UPROOTED MULTIPLE TREES...AND PEELED THE ROOF OFF A MOBILE HOME. THE TORNADO LIFTED WEST OF SCHOOL HOUSE ROAD.

*** 1 FATAL, 4 INJ *** AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 125 MPH...PATH LENGTH OF 13 MILES...AND PEAK WIDTH OF 600 YDS INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN SOUTHEAST OF DOWNTOWN LYNCHBURG. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM EASTERN MOORE COUNTY INTO FAR NW FRANKLIN COUNTY WHERE MOBILE HOMES WERE DESTROYED...MULTIPLE POWER POLES AND TREES WERE SNAPPED...AND HOMES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE. A 79 YR OLD MAN WAS KILLED EAST OF TULLAHOMA HIGHWAY ALONG UT FARM ROAD AS A MOBILE HOME WAS PICKED UP AND BLOWN 70 YDS.

WIDESPREAD WIND DAMAGE IS BEING REPORTED ALL ACROSS COLBERT COUNTY. LARGE TREES HAVE BEEN REPORTED DOWN ON CARS AND HOUSES AND ARE BLOCKING ROADWAYS. WIDESPREAD POWER OUTAGES ARE ALSO OCCURRING.
TOP OF LARGE HARDWOOD TREE BROKEN AT COVES COURT RESIDENCE. TOOK DOWN POWER LINE TO HOUSE. ALL POWER OUT IN NEIGHBORHOOD.
ROOF TORN OFF OF HR BLOCK BUILDING ON THE CORNER OF AVALON AVE AND WOODWARD.

A SHORT-LIVED TORNADO DEVELOPED ALONG A SQUALL LINE OF SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS. BASED ON GROUND SURVEYS...THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF COUNTY ROAD CR 92 IN EASTERN LAUDERDALE COUNTY. THE WORST DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG CR 76...ABOUT 1/2 MILE WEST OF STATE HIGHWAY 101...WHERE A SMALL BARN WAS NEARLY DESTROYED AND SEVERAL LARGE PINES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. ONE PINE FELL ON A HOME ALONG CR 76...CAUSING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO THE STRUCTURE. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR HWY 101.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE DAVIS/SNAKE ROAD INTERSECTION AND TRACKED NEARLY 2 MILES NORTHEAST BEFORE LIFTING NEAR FIELDING RD/CR 15 AND STANFORD RD. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED/SNAPPED...ONE WHICH FELL ON THE CORNER OF A SINGLE-STORY HOUSE. THE TORNADO SKIPPED NORTHEAST AND SNAPPED/UPROOTED ADDITIONAL TREES AROUND A RESIDENCE BETWEEN FIELDING RD/CR 15 AND DAVIS RD.
UNKNOWN DAMAGE REPORTED IN ANDERSON. SPOTTER REPORTED HEARING A ROAR.
TREES DOWN AND WIDESPREAD POWER OUTAGES. TREE ON A MOBILE HOME AT THE INTERSECTION OF 798 AND CR 235.
TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES.
WIDESPREAD WIND DAMAGE AND POWER OUTAGES. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A MOBILE HOME.

A TORNADO CROSSED HWY 99/BUCK ISLAND RD NEAR LYDIA COREY RD UPROOTING SEVERAL LARGE TREES WITH ONE FALLING ON THE SIDE OF A HOUSE. ACROSS THE STREET...A LARGE BARN WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND ALONG CROSS KEY RD...SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED/ UPROOTED. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST SNAPPING OFF THE TOPS OF SEVERAL TREES BEFORE LIFTING AGAIN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COFFMAN RD AND HWY 127. PATH LENGTH WAS 3.9 MILES.

SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED ALONG A SOUTHWEST TO NORTHEAST PATH BEGINNING ALONG THACH RD...CROSSING HWY 251 AND ENDING UP NEAR BETHEL RD AND HWY 53...JUST OVER THE LIMESTONE/MADISON COUNTY LINE. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG BETHEL RD...JUST INTO MADISON COUNTY...WHERE SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED/ UPROOTED. SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF THE ROOFS OF SEVERAL HOMES IN THIS AREA. THE TORNADO APPEARED TO LIFT JUST BEFORE HWY 53...NORTH OF BETHEL RD. PATH LENGTH WAS 4.6 MILES.
ROOFS DAMAGED AND TREES DOWN IN THE LAGRANGE MOUNTAIN AREA.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN DECATUR.

A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR MERIDIANVILLE AND SKIPPED NORTHEAST SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES ALONG ITS PATH BETWEEN HAZEL GREEN AND NEW MARKET. SEVERAL RESIDENCES OFF HARWELL CIR HAD TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED WITH SOME MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE /MAINLY TORN SIDING AND SHINGLES PULLED OFF THE ROOFS/. AS THE TORNADO CROSSED WALKER LANE NEAR MOORES MILL RD... NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED. ADDITIONAL SMALLER TREES WERE SNAPPED NEAR WALKER LANE/BUTLER RD JUST BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED.
